changes: Fixed: the Swift Onslaught (and Plummeting Descent, etc.) free charge re-roll dialog showed 'COMMAND RE-ROLL', 'Re-roll (1 CP)' and 'You have 0 CP' — players with no CP thought they could not use their free re-roll. The dialog now shows the ability name, 'Re-roll (Free)' and 'FREE — costs no CP'.

Fixed: closing any re-roll dialog with the ✕ button or ESC left the game waiting forever for a re-roll decision, blocking the rest of the Charge phase. Dismissing the dialog now counts as 'Keep Roll'.
The dice log now names the ability that was used for the free re-roll instead of always saying 'SWIFT ONSLAUGHT used!'.

releases.summary: Fixed a Movement-phase unit-list label that showed an attached leader twice. A squad with an attached CHARACTER (e.g. a Blade Champion leading a Custodian Guard squad) was listed as 'Custodian Guard Alpha + Blade Champion + Blade Champion' — the leader's name was printed twice. It now reads 'Custodian Guard Alpha + Blade Champion' once. This was purely a display bug; only one leader was ever attached.
changes: Fixed: in the Movement phase 'Units (Move / Disembark)' list, a bodyguard squad's attached leader appeared twice in the row label (e.g. '+ Blade Champion + Blade Champion'). The movement controller was appending the attached-character names to the row twice; it now appends them once.

releases.summary: Fixed Ctrl+Click not selecting a second charge target. The Eligible Targets list told you to hold Ctrl+Click to charge more than one unit, but on many setups holding Ctrl and clicking did nothing (or just moved the selection) — so you could never actually declare a multi-unit charge. Ctrl+Click now reliably adds/removes extra targets.
changes: Fixed: holding Ctrl (or Cmd) and clicking a second unit in the Eligible Targets list now toggles it in/out of the charge, so you can declare a charge against several units as the hint describes.

The charge target list no longer relies on the click carrying the Ctrl modifier (which some systems don't attach to mouse clicks) — it now also reads the live keyboard state, so Ctrl+Click works consistently across platforms.
Plain click still selects exactly one target, clicking empty space still clears the selection, and the target rows still lock once the charge is declared.

releases.summary: Multi-target shooting overhaul, part 1: declaring targets is now one click for the common case. Clicking an enemy with no weapon selected sends EVERYTHING at it; clicking with a weapon selected commits all of that weapon's eligible bearers instantly (no popup). Splitting fire is the exception you opt into: click a second target and the game offers to move some shots across. Engaged vehicles can also declare targets they are not engaged with again (10.06), and the game no longer shows error banners for assignments it auto-attempted itself.
changes: One-click targeting: click an eligible enemy with NO weapon selected to assign every usable weapon to it (a tip in the dice log explains how to split).
First assignment for a weapon commits all its eligible bearers immediately — the 'Send N of M' popup no longer appears for the common case.
Split fire by clicking a second target with the weapon selected: a 'Move how many?' picker retargets a slice of the committed bearers (no more dead-end 'No bearers can fire').

Fixed: an engaged MONSTER/VEHICLE could assign targets outside its engagement (legal per 10.06) and then have Confirm reject the whole plan with 'can only target engaged units'. Confirm now applies the same 11e shooting-type rules as assignment.

Fixed: auto-assign and quick-assign no longer dispatch assignments the rules engine would reject (which surfaced as red 'Shooting action rejected' banners the player never caused) — illegal weapons are quietly skipped and listed.

Target chips: every declared target gets a letter badge (A, B, C…) with its own color, shown on the battlefield next to the unit and reused in the weapon rows ('1→[B] Blade Champion') and the CURRENT TARGETS list — one visual identity for 'which weapon goes where'.
During resolution the chip of the target currently being shot grows and the others dim, so the battlefield itself shows whose turn it is to take fire.

NEW resolution dock: rolling dice no longer buries the battlefield under a chain of pop-up dialogs. The right panel swaps to a WEAPON RESOLUTION view — a queue showing every weapon and its target (reorder with ▲▼ before rolling), ONE big button that is always in the same place ('Roll to Hit' → 'Roll to Wound' → 'Continue to Saving Throws' → 'Next Weapon' → 'Complete Shooting'), Command Re-roll dice chips, and a Fast Roll escape. Space/Enter presses it, so you can resolve a whole volley without touching the mouse.
Completed weapons stay listed in the dock with their results ('✔ Big shoota → [A] Witchseekers (1H 1W 1 slain)'), so multi-target progress is always visible.

New 'Pauses' setting in the dock: 'Every step' (default, current behavior), 'Only decisions' (pause only when a Command Re-roll could actually change something), or 'Never' (rolls run straight through; saves and the between-weapon stop remain). Persisted between sessions.

Damage forecasts everywhere you aim: each pending weapon in the resolution queue shows its expected damage for its exact bearer slice ('4× Big shoota → [A] Witchseekers ~1.5 dmg', hover for the full attacks → hits → wounds → unsaved chain), and every quick-assign button shows the expected total if the whole unit fires at that target — previews always include the enemy's saves.

releases.summary: Fixed the split-fire bug where shooting at two different targets only rolled dice for the first one, and fixed Fast Roll re-rolling weapons it had already rolled. Multi-target shooting now reliably resolves every declared weapon and target, in both step-by-step and Fast Roll modes.
changes: Fixed: after the enemy resolved saves against your first weapon, the remaining weapons aimed at OTHER targets were dropped — the 'weapon 2 of 2' roll never happened. All declared targets now resolve in order.
Fixed: the 'Continue to Next Weapon' step was rejected with a 'Not in sequential mode' error in single-player, which dead-ended the shooting sequence and rebuilt the weapon panel mid-resolution.

Fixed: 'Fast Roll All' rolled every weapon up front but only ever applied the first weapon's wounds — then rolled the remaining weapons AGAIN (discarding their first results). Fast Roll now resolves weapon by weapon with no pauses: same dice fairness as step-by-step, just faster.
The attack summary between weapons correctly lists the remaining weapons and their targets.

The firing-order dialog now shows WHO each weapon is shooting at (green '→ target' line per weapon, with model counts when a weapon is split), and split fire announces every target instead of claiming the whole weapon fires at the first one.
Removed the large dead gray area in the roll dialog during resolution (the hidden firing-order list kept reserving its space).

releases.summary: Fixed AI fight-phase pile-in and consolidation: the game log would say an AI unit 'piles in toward enemy (N models moved)' but the tokens never actually moved on the board. The models had moved in the underlying game state — only the on-screen tokens were left behind — so combats resolved from the correct positions while the board looked wrong until the next full refresh. The tokens now follow the state immediately.
changes: Fixed: AI pile-in / consolidation moves were logged and applied to game state but the board tokens stayed at their old positions (they only snapped into place later, on a phase change or other refresh).

Root cause: the AI announces an action to the UI just before it is actually executed, so the token-sync was reading the pre-move positions and doing nothing; the sync now runs after the move is applied.
Human pile-ins were never affected — only AI-controlled units.

releases.summary: Characters attached to a squad can no longer be shot out of it. The AI (and any scripted attack) could previously target an attached leader — e.g. a Blade Champion leading a Custodian Guard squad — as if it were a separate unit, so the defender's Allocate Attacks dialog showed only the character and the wounds could not be put on the squad first. Attached leaders are now only reachable by shooting their unit: wounds are allocated to the squad's models first and the leader is reached last (or via PRECISION), per the 11th-edition attached-unit rules (19.02).
changes: Fixed: the AI no longer shoots attached characters directly — its target planning now folds attached leaders into their unit, so it shoots the squad instead.

Fixed: the rules engine now rejects any ranged attack that tries to target an attached character ('Cannot target … — attached character; shoot the bodyguard unit instead'), matching what the targeting UI already enforced for human players.
Fixed: the GRENADE stratagem can no longer pick an attached character as its target either.
When the squad leading a character is shot, the Allocate Attacks dialog shows the combined unit as before: squad models first, CHARACTER group last.
Standalone (unattached) characters are still targetable exactly as before.

releases.summary: Attached characters can now be dragged during a Charge move. Before, clicking the leader of a charging squad (e.g. the Blade Champion attached to Custodian Guard) did nothing — only the squad's own models responded — and the leader was auto-placed for you after confirming. Model picking during the charge is also much more accurate.
changes: Fixed: the attached CHARACTER of a charging unit (e.g. a Blade Champion leading Custodian Guard) could not be dragged during the charge move — clicking it silently did nothing. The leader is now draggable exactly like its squadmates, with its own reach ring, base-contact snapping, undo and Snap to Contact support.
A leader you don't drag still follows the squad automatically on confirm, and the info panel now says so instead of implying you must move it.

Fixed: clicking a charging model picked the wrong (or no) model depending on zoom — the hit test used a fixed screen-pixel radius. It now uses each model's real base size on the board and picks the nearest model under the cursor, so tightly packed squads select correctly.
The whole attached unit (squad + leader) is now checked as one unit for charge coherency, so a leader can't be dragged somewhere that splits it from its squad.

releases.summary: Leaders now pile in and consolidate WITH their unit. A Character attached to a bodyguard squad (e.g. a Blade Champion leading Custodian Guard) no longer appears as a separate unit in the Fight phase's Pile In and Consolidate steps — the picker shows one entry ('Custodian Guard + Blade Champion') and moving it lets you drag every model of the attached unit, leader included, in the one 3" move.
changes: Fixed: attached Characters (Blade Champions, Shield-Captains, Warbosses, etc.) were listed as separate units in the Pile In step at the start of the Fight phase and in the Consolidate step at its end — per the core rules (19.03) an attached unit moves as ONE unit.
The Pile In / Consolidate unit pickers now show a single entry per attached unit, named 'Bodyguard + Leader'.
Picking the unit lets you drag the leader's model too — same 3" cap, closer-to-enemy, coherency and base-contact rules, applied to the whole attached unit together.
A bodyguard whose LEADER is the only model in engagement range now correctly counts as engaged for pile-in/consolidate eligibility.
The AI moves attached leaders together with their unit when it piles in or consolidates.
Consolidation modes (ongoing/engaging/objective) are judged on the whole attached unit, and an Engaging Consolidation led by the leader's model still forces the new fight.

releases.summary: Fight phase: the 'Select Unit to Fight' picker no longer tells you your opponent 'has no eligible units' when their engaged units are simply waiting for the Remaining Combats step. During the Fights First step it now explains that the opponent has no FIGHTS FIRST units and will select once your Fights First units have fought.
changes: Fixed: during the Fights First step the picker said 'Player X has no eligible units. Select all remaining units in turn.' even when the opponent's engaged units (e.g. Lootas / Burna Boyz charged by Custodians) were owed a Remaining Combats fight. It now reads 'Player X has no Fights First units. Select your Fights First units in turn — Player X will then select in Remaining Combats.', and the blanket 'no eligible units' message only appears when the opponent truly has nothing left to fight.

releases.summary: Charge target selection is now predictable: clicking a target in the ELIGIBLE TARGETS list selects exactly that one target (no more accidentally declaring a charge against two units), and deliberately charging several units is now a real, clearly-signposted feature — a highlighted callout under the target list spells out that you hold Ctrl+Click to charge more than one unit, the Declare button shows how many units you are about to charge, and the list locks once the charge is declared.
changes: Fixed: clicking a second charge target used to silently KEEP the first one too — the game declared a charge against both units while only the last one stayed highlighted. A plain click now selects exactly the clicked target.

Multi-unit charges are now explicit and impossible to miss: a gold-bordered callout under the ELIGIBLE TARGETS list tells you to hold Ctrl+Click (Cmd on Mac) to charge more than one unit, and its wording updates as you go — once you pick a first target it actively prompts 'hold Ctrl+Click another target' to charge both.
The Declare Charge button shows the target count when charging more than one unit (e.g. 'Declare Charge (2 targets)'), and the step guidance calls out that ALL selected targets must be reached.
Clicking empty space in the target list clears the selection.
Deselecting targets actually works now (it previously did nothing under the hood).
Once a charge is declared its target rows lock until the charge is resolved, so stray clicks can't desync the display from the declared charge.
Fixed: a declared charge's target list could be silently emptied by later UI resets (selecting another unit before rolling) — the declaration is now stored as its own copy.

releases.summary: Mouse-wheel zoom now only acts when the cursor is over the game board. Scrolling while hovering a menu or panel (unit list, game log, HUD bar, dialogs) no longer zooms the board underneath — the wheel belongs to the menu you're pointing at.
changes: Fixed: scrolling a side panel, the game log, or any other menu no longer also zooms the board — wheel zoom is skipped whenever the cursor is over a UI element.
Scrolling over the open board still zooms in/out anchored on the cursor, exactly as before.

releases.summary: The Fight phase 'Select Unit to Fight' picker no longer lists Fights First units twice. Units that fight first (chargers and Fights First ability carriers) appeared under BOTH the FIGHTS_FIRST section and REMAINING_COMBATS, reading as if they fought twice — now every unit is listed in exactly one section. The picker also shows proper unit names throughout.
changes: Fixed: Fights First units (charged units and ability carriers) were duplicated into the REMAINING_COMBATS list of the fight picker; each unit now appears only under the step it will actually fight in.
The fight picker now uses distinguishing display names ('Warboss Alpha', 'Blade Champion Beta') so same-named units are tellable apart, matching the rest of the game.
Fixed: the opposing player's units in the fight picker rendered as raw internal ids (e.g. 'U_CUSTODIAN_GUARD_B') instead of their names.

releases.summary: Charge moves now support multi-select: Shift+drag a box (or Ctrl+click / Ctrl+A) over your charging models and drag any selected model to move the whole group at once, just like in the Movement phase.
changes: Charge phase: Shift+drag on empty board rubber-bands a selection box over the charging unit's models; Ctrl+click toggles single models; Ctrl+A selects all; Esc clears the selection.

Dragging any selected model moves the whole group together, with each model's move checked against the charge roll, overlaps, board edge and the 'end closer to a target' rule (all-or-nothing: an illegal group move moves nothing).
Selected models show pulsing rings and the group shows live green/red ghost previews while dragging; the selection stays active for further hops until the charge roll is spent.

Fixed charge-move model pick-up using a fixed screen-pixel hit radius: at zoomed-out camera scales every token grabbed clicks from over 2 inches away, making it easy to pick up the wrong model. Hit-testing now uses each model's real base size on the board.

releases.summary: Shooting dice readouts are now honest about cover and re-rolls: the 'need X+' hit label shows the real (cover-adjusted) target, wound re-rolls of 1 are annotated in the roll dialog instead of looking like a 1 that counted, and the Forecast panel accounts for cover and re-roll abilities like Stand Vigil.
changes: Fixed: the hit-roll line said 'need 2+' even when the target's Benefit of Cover (11th ed 13.08) worsened the roll to 3+ — the dice were already being judged at 3+, only the label lied. It now reads e.g. 'need 3+ (cover)' in the roll dialog, game log, dice history and combat summaries.

Fixed: a wound roll of 1 that an ability re-rolled (e.g. Custodian Guard's Stand Vigil, twin-linked) looked like it had counted as a wound — the step-by-step roll dialog and the next-weapon summary now show the re-roll annotation (e.g. '(1→6)') like the main game log already did.

Fixed: the Forecast panel ignored the target's cover (over-predicting hits) and ability-granted re-rolls such as Stand Vigil (under-predicting wounds). It now models both and tags them ('Cover', 'RR 1s Wnd').

releases.summary: 'Snap to Contact' in the Charge phase now always does something useful. Before, if your charge roll was too short for any model to reach base contact, clicking the button moved nothing (it only tried spots right up against the target), which read as the button being broken. Now every charging model that can't reach base contact is moved as close to the target as its charge distance legally allows.
changes: Snap to Contact: models that can reach base contact still snap into contact exactly as before.

Models that CAN'T reach contact now advance straight at the nearest target model, stopping just inside the 1" engagement band when the roll reaches it, or at their full charge distance when it doesn't — instead of standing still.
If the straight lane is blocked (squadmate, wall, terrain cost), the button sweeps nearby approach angles and shorter moves to find the closest legal spot.
Auto-placed spots now also avoid ending within 1" of an enemy unit you did NOT declare the charge against, so the Confirm step can't be rejected for an illegal engagement.
The charge panel message now says what happened, e.g. 'No model could reach base contact — all moved as close as possible. Confirm or adjust'.

releases.summary: Fixed a Fight-phase soft-lock: a unit that charged but couldn't reach anyone (e.g. a Blade Champion whose pile-in falls short) used to open the 'Assign Attacks' dialog with zero eligible targets and no way to skip, freezing the game. Its fight now simply ends — the game logs it and moves on to the next unit.
changes: A unit selected to fight with no enemies within Engagement Range after its pile-in (11e Overrun fight, 12.06) now ends its activation automatically instead of opening an empty attack-assignment dialog — the fight sequence continues normally.
The Game Log explains what happened: 'no enemies in engagement range, fight ends without attacks'.

Safety net: if the Assign Attacks dialog ever opens with no targets, it now disables the attack buttons, explains why, and offers an 'End Fight (No Targets)' button that skips the unit — no more being trapped in the dialog.

releases.summary: Deployment placement ghosts now show the model's top-down sprite, not just the pulsing base outline. The single-model placement ghost already did this, but when you placed a squad using the Spread or Tight formation, or nudged an already-placed model, the preview ghosts only drew the empty base outline. Every deployment ghost that has bundled/custom art (Custodian Guard, Stormboyz, tanks, etc.) now renders that art inside the translucent base so you can see exactly what — and which way — you're placing.
changes: Fixed: Spread/Tight formation deployment ghosts now show each model's facing sprite instead of just the base outline.
Fixed: the reposition ghost (when moving an already-placed model during deployment) now shows the model's sprite too.
Units without dedicated art are unchanged — they keep the letter counter + facing arrow.

releases.summary: The orange 12" charge-range indicator now shows the unit's TRUE charge threat area. It used to be a single circle drawn from the unit's midpoint, but a charge only needs a target within 12" of ANY model — so for spread-out units the circle disagreed with the eligible-target list (targets listed as in range could sit visibly outside the circle). The indicator is now one dashed outline hugging the whole unit: the union of every model's 12" reach, measured from each base edge.
changes: Charge phase: selecting a unit now draws the 12" threat boundary around EVERY model in the unit (one merged dashed outline), not a circle from the unit's centre.

The boundary is measured from each model's base edge (edge-to-edge, matching how charge eligibility is actually checked), so a target is chargeable exactly when its base touches the orange area — the drawing and the Eligible Targets list now always agree.
Works with mixed base types (circular, oval and rectangular bases all project their real 12" reach).
The green per-model reach rings shown after the 2D6 roll are unchanged.

releases.summary: The menu scroll speed is now adjustable. Settings › Controls has a new 'Menu Scroll Speed' slider (10%–100% of the default), so you can dial mouse-wheel / trackpad scrolling to whatever feels right. Your choice is saved between sessions.
changes: Added a 'Menu Scroll Speed' slider under Settings › Controls › Mouse (default 40%).
The setting applies live to all menus/panels and persists across restarts.
Board zoom and number spinners remain unaffected.

releases.summary: Scrolling through menus and panels with the mouse wheel or trackpad is now slower and easier to control. Previously the wheel/two-finger scroll flew past what you were looking at; menu scrolling now moves at 40% of the old speed everywhere in the game.
changes: Reduced mouse-wheel and trackpad scroll speed across all menus, lists and panels (main menu, army/unit panels, dialogs, the game log, etc.).
Board zoom (wheel over the battlefield) and number spinners are unchanged — only content scrolling was slowed.

releases.summary: Fixed the confusing empty 'FIRING ORDER' box in the shooting roll dialog. When a unit fires a single weapon type, the step-by-step roll dialog now shows the weapon being fired under a 'WEAPON' header; with multiple weapon types, the ordering list disappears completely once the sequence starts instead of leaving an empty box behind.
changes: Single-weapon shooting: the roll dialog now shows the firing weapon (name, model count and stats) under a 'WEAPON' header instead of an empty 'FIRING ORDER' area.
Multi-weapon shooting: once 'Start Sequence' is pressed, the whole firing-order section is hidden during resolution (previously an empty 'FIRING ORDER' header and box remained on screen).
The dice log now says 'Single weapon - rolling step by step...' when only one weapon type fires, instead of the misleading 'Multiple weapon types detected - choose firing order...'.

releases.summary: The Declare Battle Formations screen now tells identically-named units apart. Before, if your army had several units with the same name (e.g. six Custodian Guard squads, three Blade Champions), the leader-attachment dropdowns, warlord picker, transport/reserves lists and summary all just read 'Custodian Guard (5 models)' over and over, so you couldn't tell which squad you were attaching a leader to. Every unit now shows its distinguishing suffix — 'Custodian Guard Alpha', 'Custodian Guard Beta', etc. — matching the labels used everywhere else in the game.
changes: Leader-attachment dropdowns now list bodyguard units by their full display name (e.g. 'Custodian Guard Alpha (5 models)') instead of the shared base name, so duplicate squads are distinguishable.
The character labels beside each dropdown, the Warlord picker, the transport-embarkation and strategic-reserves checkboxes, and the on-screen summary all use the same distinguishing names.
Units with a unique name are unaffected — they still show their normal name.

releases.summary: Line of sight now follows the 11th-edition terrain rules correctly: units standing ON a terrain piece can be seen and shot (their own terrain area no longer blocks the sight-line check), and when a unit truly can't be targeted the game explains WHY — including the Hidden rule (infantry lurking in dense terrain is only visible within 15") instead of a blanket 'No line of sight'.
changes: Fixed: units standing on a terrain area (e.g. the slanted centre strips) were wrongly untargetable — the halves of a linked terrain area and its parented wall/gantry features now count as ONE area for the Obscuring rule (13.10), so a model within it is never blocked by its own area.
Fixed: 'within a terrain area' now uses any part of the model's base (the rules' Within convention), not just the base centre — models straddling an area edge are seen (and Hidden) correctly.
Fixed: a model standing in/on a structure (catwalk, ruin footprint) is no longer blocked by the piece it occupies.
Fixed: Gone to Ground no longer subtracts 3" of detection range for terrain the target itself is standing in.
Solid walls (13.11) still block ground-level sight lines — being on terrain exposes you, but a wall between you stays opaque.

The shooting panel now explains ineligible targets precisely: 'X is Hidden (dense terrain, hasn't shot recently) — visible only within 15" detection range; your closest model is N" away' or 'terrain blocks every sight line'.
The no-eligible-targets toast points at the Dice Log breakdown and mentions the Hidden rule.

releases.summary: Board zoom now follows your mouse. Zooming with + / - keeps the point under the cursor fixed instead of drifting toward the top-left corner, and you can now zoom with the mouse scroll wheel too — scroll up to zoom in, down to zoom out, both centred on the cursor. Scrolling over a side panel still scrolls that panel, and during deployment the wheel keeps rotating the model you are placing. The wheel-zoom is listed under a new Mouse section in Settings > Controls.
changes: + / - zoom (and controller trigger zoom) now keeps the spot under the cursor fixed instead of drifting toward the top-left corner of the screen; controller zoom anchors on the centre of the screen since a gamepad has no cursor.
Mouse scroll wheel now zooms the board: up = in, down = out, anchored on the cursor.
Scrolling while the cursor is over a UI panel (game log, unit list) scrolls that panel rather than zooming the board.
During deployment the wheel still rotates the model being placed — zoom only kicks in outside placement.
Added a Mouse section to Settings > Controls listing 'Zoom In / Out — Mouse Wheel', and noted the wheel on the in-game shortcut overlay (press ?).
Zoom amount, speed and limits are unchanged — only the point the view zooms around has changed.

releases.summary: Deep Strike reserves now follow the core rule that EVERY model in a unit must have Deep Strike. On the Declare Battle Formations screen a bodyguard with attached leaders is only offered Deep Strike when the bodyguard AND every attached leader has it. Previously the screen used OR logic — if the squad or any attached character had Deep Strike the whole unit was offered it — so attaching a Deep Strike character (such as a Blade Champion) to a unit that lacks Deep Strike wrongly let the combined unit Deep Strike. Whole-unit grants such as the Ork Tellyporta enhancement, which give Deep Strike to every model in the bearer's unit, still make the entire unit eligible.
changes: The formations screen now only offers Deep Strike for a unit when every model in it — the bodyguard and every attached leader — has the Deep Strike ability, matching the core rule.
Attaching a Deep Strike character to a unit that does not have Deep Strike no longer lets the combined unit Deep Strike; it is offered plain Strategic Reserves instead.
Whole-unit Deep Strike grants (e.g. the Ork Tellyporta enhancement) still make the entire combined unit eligible for Deep Strike, as before.
This also removes an inconsistency: the declaration screen now agrees with how the reserve is actually resolved later in the game.

releases.summary: On-foot Adeptus Custodes units can now Deep Strike as they should. In the 11th edition data almost the entire Custodes range (Custodian Guard, Allarus Custodians, Shield-Captains, Trajann Valoris, and more) has the Deep Strike ability, but the army lists only carried it on the Blade Champion. That meant plain Custodian Guard squads were stuck with plain Strategic Reserves, and a squad only 'gained' Deep Strike when a Blade Champion was attached to it. The Deep Strike ability has been restored to the units that are supposed to have it, so every eligible squad now offers Deep Strike in the Declare Battle Formations screen. Jetbikes, vehicles, dreadnoughts and the Sisters of Silence (Prosecutors/Witchseekers) correctly still do not Deep Strike.
changes: Custodian Guard, Allarus Custodians, Shield-Captains and Trajann Valoris now have their Deep Strike ability in the Adeptus Custodes army lists, matching the 11th edition datasheets.
In the Declare Battle Formations screen these units now offer 'Deep Strike' reserves on their own, instead of only 'Strategic Reserves'.
Fixes the inconsistency where a Custodian Guard squad showed 'Deep Strike' only while a Blade Champion was attached, and 'Strategic Reserves' otherwise — every eligible squad is treated the same now.
Mounted units (Shield-Captain on Dawneagle Jetbike, Vertus Praetors), vehicles/dreadnoughts, and the Anathema Psykana Sisters of Silence remain non–Deep Strike, as per the rules.

releases.summary: Fixed deployment breaking after loading a saved game. If you saved during (or just before) the Deployment phase and loaded that save, every attempt to deploy a unit was rejected with 'Unit is not available for deployment' — the AI opponent would dump its army into Strategic Reserves until the 50% points cap and then skip its remaining units entirely, leaving it with nothing on the board (reported with the Battlewagons Ork list vs the Competitive AI). Loading a save no longer breaks deployment for either player.
changes: Fixed every deployment being rejected as 'Unit is not available for deployment' after loading a save made around the Deployment phase. Saved games store numbers in a format that loads back as decimals, and the deployment legality check compared those decimals too strictly, so no unit ever qualified as deployable.

Because each failed deployment fell back to Strategic Reserves, the AI would silently bank its army into reserves until the 50% reserves points limit was hit and then skip the rest of its units — the AI now deploys normally after a load.
Loaded games now restore unit status and ownership (plus turn/phase bookkeeping) as proper whole numbers, protecting other rules checks from the same decimals-after-load problem.

releases.summary: Fixed terrain wrongly slowing Infantry during charges. Units that can move through ruins (Infantry) were being charged a flat 2" per 'difficult ground' terrain piece on top of the actual distance when charging — the panel showed things like 'Effective: 16.1" (10.1" - 6.0" terrain)', tiny drags read as many extra inches, and charges that should easily succeed were rejected as out of range. The official 11th-edition terrain layouts mark all their large ruin areas as difficult ground, so this hit almost every charge fought in or through ruins. The Movement phase already let Infantry pass freely; the Charge phase now matches it.
changes: Infantry charging through or within ruins/difficult-ground areas no longer pay any added distance — the charge tracker now shows only the real inches you dragged, and the 'terrain' penalty line no longer appears for them.

Fixes charges failing with 'Invalid position! Must be within X"' (or failing outright at the roll) when the charger stood inside one of the big area ruins that the official 11e layouts tag as difficult ground — each overlapping piece was silently adding 2".
Units that genuinely cannot traverse the terrain (Vehicles, Monsters over ruins) are unchanged and still pay climb and difficult-ground penalties; Fly units are unchanged too.
Charge and Movement phases now use the same rule: terrain a unit can move through never slows that unit.

releases.summary: Fixed hovering and right-clicking your own models doing nothing after you deployed them. Units you placed during deployment (and reinforcements arriving from reserves) were left on the board as internal 'placement preview' containers, invisible to the hover tooltip, the right-click Change Color / Change Label / Unit Stats menu, and some movement visual updates — while AI-deployed and save-loaded units worked fine, which is why only your own models seemed dead. Confirmed units are now converted to the same standard token form as every other model on the board.
changes: Hovering a model you deployed yourself now shows the info tooltip again (unit summary, and the per-model profile block where applicable).
Right-clicking your own models now opens the context menu again, so you can change unit colors and labels after deploying.
Also applies to units arriving from Strategic Reserves / deep strike mid-game — their tokens now behave identically to every other token.
Loading a save was never affected; this only concerned units placed by hand in the current session.

releases.summary: The defender is now in control when their models are attacked. When your units are targeted you get the reactive stratagem window (no more 5-second auto-decline, and it now works online and against the AI), YOU click to roll your own saves, you can spend 1 CP on a Command Re-roll of one failed save die, and when models die YOU click which bases are removed from the board. This applies in the shooting and fight phases, in local play, online multiplayer, and when the AI is attacking you.
changes: Saves are always rolled by the owner of the target unit: the allocation window now waits for your 'Roll Saves' click instead of instantly auto-resolving single-group squads, and melee saves are interactive by default too (the old auto behaviour is now an opt-in setting).

You choose your casualties: after saves are rolled you click exactly which bases are removed (wounded models are locked in per the rules, and an 'Auto-pick For Me' button keeps fast play available). Which base you keep matters for objective control.
Command Re-roll on saves: if you have 1 CP you can re-roll one failed save die before damage is applied — in both shooting and melee.

Reactive stratagems fixed online: the attacker's machine no longer silently declines on your behalf — the decision window appears on the defender's screen and the attacker waits. The 5-second auto-decline countdown is gone; the window stays open until you choose.

The AI now pauses its attacks for you: when the AI targets your units you get the reactive stratagem window, then roll your own saves and pick your own casualties, exactly as against a human attacker.

The 'Computer allocates wounds (auto-remove models)' setting now defaults OFF (existing installs are migrated once); turn it back on in Settings if you prefer the old hands-off behaviour in local games.

releases.summary: Models being deployed now face the enemy by default and show their sprite while you place them. Previously every model's artwork pointed 'up' the board regardless of which side you were on, and during deployment you only saw a plain base with no indication of facing. Now the deployment ghost renders the model's top-down sprite, oriented toward the opponent's board edge, and it turns live as you rotate the model — so what you see while placing matches how the model will sit on the table.
changes: Deployed models now start facing the opponent's board edge instead of always pointing up the board; the facing is worked out from your deployment zone toward the enemy's, so it is correct for every deployment map.
While deploying, the placement preview now shows the model's actual sprite (or a facing arrow for units without dedicated art) so you can see which way it is pointing before you drop it.
Rotating a model during deployment (Q/E or the mouse wheel) now visibly turns its sprite — especially useful for circular bases, where the base alone gave no clue which way it faced.
You can still rotate freely to any angle; rotating simply overrides the default enemy-facing orientation.

releases.summary: Squad leaders and special models now look different from their rank-and-file. When a unit contains models with different loadouts — a Boss Nob leading Stormboyz or Warbikers, a Runtherd bossing a Gretchin mob — that model now shows its own top-down sprite on the board (a power klaw for the Nobs, a grabba-stikk and slaver's cap for the Runtherd) and, when you hover over it, a datasheet-style block listing that specific model's role and weapons. Applied to the Recon Stompa (Speedwaaagh!) Orks list. Custodes squads were checked: their Sister Superiors carry the same weapons as the squad in the current data, so they intentionally share the squad sprite.
changes: Different models in the same unit can now show different sprites. The one Boss Nob in a Stormboyz or Warbikers squad, and the Runtherd in a Gretchin mob, are drawn with distinct top-down art so you can tell the leader apart at a glance.

Hovering a model now shows that model's own profile: its role name (e.g. 'Boss Nob') and the weapons it actually carries, in addition to the unit summary. Rank-and-file and leader loadouts are listed separately.
Deployed leader/special models also keep the existing coloured ring + short letter on their base, so mixed squads are readable even before you hover.
New Boss Nob (foot), Boss Nob on Warbike, and Runtherd sprites were added; ordinary squad members are unchanged and keep the shared squad sprite.
Units with a single uniform loadout are completely unchanged — this only affects squads that genuinely contain different model profiles.

releases.summary: Fixed the AI opponent skipping its Pile In moves in the Fight phase. In 11th edition Pile In is a shared step — you pile in all your eligible units, then your opponent piles in all of theirs, and only then does the fighting begin. On your turn the AI's engaged units were never getting their pile-in: the game correctly handed the Pile In step to the AI, but an internal 'whose turn is it?' check wrongly decided it was still your turn, so the AI sat idle and the step could stall on the AI's half. The AI now plays its half of the Pile In (and Consolidate) step correctly.
changes: During your turn's Fight phase, after you finish your Pile In moves the AI opponent now actually piles in its engaged units toward your models before the fighting starts, as the 11th-edition rules require.

Root cause: the AI's 'does a human currently owe this fight-phase decision?' guard fell through to the fight-selection order — which starts with the active player (you) — during the AI's OWN Pile In / Consolidate half, so the AI was told to wait and never moved. It now correctly recognises when the AI owns that half.
No change to your own pile-ins, or to how fights are resolved after everyone has piled in.

releases.summary: Fixed attached characters (e.g. a Blade Champion leading Custodian Guard) ending up stacked on top of one of their own bodyguard models after the unit made an Advance/Normal move or a Charge. The leader rode along by a fixed offset with no collision check, so re-arranging the squad could drop it directly onto another model; it is now nudged to the nearest clear spot so bases never overlap.
changes: When a led unit moves or charges and you re-position its bodyguard models, the attached character no longer lands on top of a bodyguard model — it is automatically shifted to the closest non-overlapping position next to where it would have gone.
Applies to Normal moves, Advances, Fall Backs and Charges. The character still moves with its unit and stays in coherency; only the final overlap is corrected.
Multiple attached characters riding the same unit are also kept from stacking on each other.

releases.summary: Charge phase now shows each model's actual charge-move reach while you drag. After you roll the 2D6 charge distance, the single 12" range circle is replaced with a per-model reach ring (radius = the distance you rolled) centred on every charging model, so you can see exactly how far each model is allowed to move instead of a 12" circle that no longer applies.
changes: After the charge roll, the board draws one green reach ring per charging model, each sized to the distance you actually rolled, labelled e.g. '8" charge move (each model)'.
The orange 12" charge-threat circle is now only shown before the roll (while picking targets); once you're dragging models it is cleared so it no longer sits there implying you can move 12".
Rings are anchored on each model's starting position and stay put as a fixed reference while you drag; the right-panel Used/Left readout still tracks the live remaining distance.

releases.summary: Fixed the Consolidate step refusing to move a unit that was on or near a large terrain objective — it now correctly offers the 'move onto the objective' consolidation instead of telling you 'no consolidation mode applies — the unit cannot move'.
changes: Objective consolidation is now offered whenever your unit is within 3" of an objective (per the rule), measured — for a terrain objective such as the central ruin — to the objective's AREA rather than to the marker's centre point. Previously a unit on or beside a big central objective could be several inches from the exact marker point and was wrongly told it had no consolidation move available.

The consolidation move itself still has to end within range of the objective (a base on the terrain area, or within 3" of a normal marker); only the check for whether the option is offered was widened. Open-ground objectives are unchanged (still 3" from the marker).

releases.summary: Clearer Fight-phase button during Pile In: the top-right action button now reads 'Finish Pile Ins' (and 'Finish Consolidations') while you're in those steps, instead of the misleading 'End Fight Phase'. Clicking it during Pile In no longer pops the wrong 'these units haven't fought' warning — those units still fight, so there was nothing to warn about. The warning now only appears when you actually end the Fight step.
changes: The top-right phase button is now labelled for the current Fight-phase step: '[Enter] Finish Pile Ins' during the Pile In step, '[Enter] Finish Consolidations' during the Consolidate step, and '[Enter] End Fight Phase' during the fight itself.

Finishing your Pile In (or Consolidation) moves via that button no longer shows the 'N unit(s) have not yet fought — are you sure?' confirmation. That pop-up was left over from an old code path and was misleading: ending a Pile In forfeits no fights, so your units still get to fight.
The 'unfought units' confirmation still appears when you genuinely end the Fight step early, where it correctly warns you about forfeiting fights.

releases.summary: Internal Fight-phase cleanup: the game now runs a single, current-edition Fight-phase code path — the old 10th-edition pile-in/consolidate/fight-ordering leftovers were removed so the two rule sets can no longer shadow each other. This is invisible in normal play. The one small on-screen consequence: a unit forced to 'fight last' by an ability now appears in the ordinary 'Remaining Combats' group of the fight-selection list rather than a separate 'Fights Last' group — matching the current rules, which resolve it as a normal remaining combat and have no distinct Fights Last step. Board colouring of engaged units (including the fights-last tint) is unchanged.
changes: The Fight phase is now driven entirely by the current-edition fight sequencer; the parallel 10th-edition fight-ordering state (Subphase / tier lists / legacy fight queue) was removed. No change to how fights play out.

A 'fights last' unit is now listed under 'Remaining Combats' in the fight-selection dialog instead of a separate 'Fights Last' section, matching the current rules (there is no separate Fights Last step).

releases.summary: Tightened the base-to-base contact tolerance during Pile In / Consolidate — the game was treating models up to a quarter-inch apart as already touching, locking them from moving even with a clearly visible gap on the board.
changes: The 'model is already in base contact and cannot move' lock (the B2B icon during Pile In) now only triggers within 0.1" of true base contact, down from 0.25" — matches what the tabletop actually looks like instead of locking models with a visible gap.
Applied consistently everywhere base contact is judged: the Pile In/Consolidate move lock, the melee fight-eligibility base-contact chain, and the AI's own pile-in/consolidate movement logic.

releases.summary: Fixed Charge-phase model rotation not showing up on the actual unit token — only the green drag ghost was turning.
changes: When you rotated a model (Q/E, or right-click-drag) while dragging it into a charge, the preview ghost turned correctly but the real token on the board stayed facing its original direction, both mid-drag and after dropping it.
The token-visual updater was reaching into the wrong child node to apply the rotation, so the repaint never happened; it now updates the actual on-screen token directly, matching the ghost.

releases.summary: Fight-phase cleanup: rejected fight actions now tell you WHY, and two dead leftover buttons were removed. Previously, when a fight action was refused (for example, clicking a unit to fight before the Pile In step had finished), the game showed a red 'Fight action failed: Unknown error' banner that hid the real reason. It now shows the actual rule, e.g. 'The Pile In step (12.02) must finish before units are selected to fight'. The old per-unit 'Pile In' and 'Consolidate' buttons in the fight side panel — a leftover from the 10th-edition rules where those were part of each unit's activation — have been removed; in the current rules pile-in and consolidation are handled by their own dedicated steps, and those two buttons were permanently greyed out and did nothing.
changes: Rejected fight actions now show the real reason instead of a generic 'Unknown error'. The same fix also restores the real message for rejected Shooting and Charge actions.

Removed the non-functional per-unit 'Pile In' / 'Consolidate' buttons from the Fight controls panel — pile-in and consolidation are driven by their own dedicated on-screen steps, so those buttons were dead controls that could never be clicked.

releases.summary: Models on non-circular bases (bikes, and vehicles like the Caladius Grav-tank or a rectangular Battlewagon) can now be pivoted about their centre while piling in — exactly the way you already rotate them in the Movement phase. Right-click-drag a model during pile-in to turn it, or use the Q/E rotate keys, so an oval or rectangular base can be oriented to reach the enemy. As in the Movement phase, the 2" pivot cost is counted against the model's 3" pile-in move, so a model that turns can only shuffle 1" of ground; round infantry bases don't need to pivot and are unaffected.
changes: Pile in: right-click-drag (or press Q / E) to rotate a model on a non-circular base about its centre, matching the Movement-phase rotation controls — useful for pointing a bike or tank so its long base reaches into combat.
The pivot costs 2" out of the 3" pile-in move (Pariah Nexus rule), so a model that turns can still move up to 1"; the move is rejected if a turn-plus-shuffle would exceed that reduced allowance.
Only bases that actually need to pivot can be turned — any non-round base, or a round base over 32mm on a flying stem for a Vehicle; standard round infantry bases are left as-is.
The new facing is saved with the model and applied when the pile-in is confirmed, so a bike/tank keeps the way you turned it. Rotation also works the same way in the end-of-phase Consolidate step.

releases.summary: AI actions no longer pop up in a separate floating window — they're now shown only in the Game Log. Previously, when the computer took its turn (or reacted during yours), a small 'AI Actions' ticker appeared in the bottom-right corner duplicating what the AI was thinking and doing, then faded away. That information was useful but easy to miss and cluttered the board. All of it — the AI's reasoning, the actions it takes, and (in AI-vs-AI spectator games) the per-phase summaries — now flows into the left-hand Game Log panel like every other event, so there's a single place to follow the whole battle and nothing pops up over the battlefield.
changes: Removed the separate bottom-right 'AI Actions' pop-up overlay. The AI's reasoning and actions already appear in the Game Log, so this window was redundant and could briefly cover the board.
AI-vs-AI spectator per-phase summaries (e.g. 'Units moved: 3, Units advanced: 1') are now written into the Game Log as an entry instead of only showing in the old pop-up.
No information is lost — everything the pop-up showed is in the Game Log panel, which you can filter (AI, Move, Shoot, etc.) and scroll back through at any time.

releases.summary: Single-weapon shooting now steps through the dice roll-by-roll, just like a unit firing several weapon types already did. When you select a unit, pick a target and confirm, the game now opens a pop-up that rolls to hit and PAUSES so you can read the result (and spend Command Re-roll on a die), then rolls to wound and PAUSES again, before handing off to the defender's saving throws. Previously a single-weapon shot rolled its hits and wounds together in one go and jumped straight to saves, so there was no window to react between the hit and wound rolls. A 'Fast Roll' button in the pop-up resolves the rest at once whenever you don't want to step through.
changes: Shooting with a single weapon type now shows the same step-by-step roll pop-up that multi-weapon shooting uses: Roll to Hit → (pause) → Roll to Wound → (pause) → Saving throws.
Command Re-roll (1 CP) is now offered on the hit roll AND the wound roll of a single-weapon shot — click a die in the pop-up to re-roll it (once per phase), the same as when firing multiple weapons.

Added a 'Fast Roll' button to the roll pop-up: from either pause it resolves the remaining rolls immediately and goes straight to saves, so you're never forced to click through when you don't need to.
Firing several weapon types is unchanged — you still get the weapon-ordering pop-up first, then choose Fast Roll or step through the sequence.

releases.summary: Fixed shooting-phase target assignment by clicking on an enemy unit not filling the 'Current Targets' list. When you selected a weapon and clicked an enemy that had several of your models able to fire at it, the Split Fire picker opened, you chose how many to send, and the assignment was actually made — but the 'Current Targets' panel stayed empty and the 'Confirm Targets' button stayed greyed out, so it looked like nothing happened and you couldn't proceed. Assigning via the 'Quick Assign' buttons worked because it refreshed the panel directly. Now the panel and buttons refresh after every target you commit, whichever way you assigned it.
changes: Fixed: clicking an enemy unit to assign shooting targets now fills the 'Current Targets' list and enables 'Confirm Targets'. Previously, assignments made through the Split Fire picker (or the Devastating Wounds choice prompt) committed silently — the target was assigned but the on-screen list and buttons never updated.

The fix refreshes the assignment UI at the moment the assignment is actually committed, so it works the same whether the picker/choice dialog was involved or not — matching how the 'Quick Assign' and 'Apply to All' buttons already behaved.

releases.summary: Moved the AI Turn Summary into the game log instead of a floating pop-up. After each computer turn a summary of what the AI did (units moved, reinforcements arrived, charges declared, units fought, etc.) used to appear as a separate window pinned to the right-hand side of the screen — but it was drawn on top of the existing menus and combat log, so it overlapped them and was awkward to read. That pop-up is gone. The same summary now appears as a single card in the right-hand game log, labelled 'AI Turn Summary', with the full per-phase breakdown shown by default so you can read it at a glance — plus a 'hide details' toggle if you want to collapse it. It filters with the log's 'AI' category and can be clicked to review the board at that point in the game.
changes: The post-turn 'AI Turn Summary' no longer appears as a separate pop-up window that covered the existing menus.

The same information (units moved, reinforcements, charges, fights, stratagems, notable moments) is now logged as an 'AI Turn Summary' card in the right-hand game log, expanded by default so the full per-phase breakdown is visible.
Use the card's 'hide details' toggle to collapse it; it hides/shows with the log's 'AI' filter like other AI entries.

releases.summary: The charge phase's 'UNITS THAT CAN CHARGE' list now only shows units that actually have an enemy within 12" — i.e. a target that a charge could reach. Previously the list showed every unit that was allowed to charge, even ones with no enemy anywhere near them, so you'd select a unit expecting to charge and find an empty 'ELIGIBLE TARGETS' box. The list is now filtered so every unit shown has at least one reachable target, which is far less confusing.
changes: Charge phase: 'UNITS THAT CAN CHARGE' now lists only units with at least one enemy within 12" (a target a charge could reach). Units eligible to charge but with no enemy in range are hidden.
The 'ELIGIBLE TARGETS' list a selected unit shows is now guaranteed non-empty — selecting any listed unit always presents at least one target.
The 'X eligible' charge counter in the right panel now counts only these in-range units, so it matches the list you see.

releases.summary: Charge moves can now be made in multiple steps, exactly like Movement-phase moves. Previously, once you dragged a charging model and let go, that model was 'done' — you couldn't pick it up again, so there was no way to route it around a wall, terrain, or your own models and still use the rest of your charge distance. Now each charging model can be dragged as many times as you like; every drag adds to the total distance used and the model can keep moving until it reaches engagement range or spends its charge roll. Only 'Confirm Charge Moves' finalises the charge.
changes: Multi-step charge movement: drag a charging model, release, then drag it again — each hop accumulates toward the model's charge distance (the 'Used'/'Left' readout updates across hops), so you can steer around obstacles instead of being limited to a single straight drag.

A charging model that has already been placed can be picked up and moved again before you confirm; the total path is capped at the charge roll, and an over-budget drag is rejected (the model snaps back) just like in the Movement phase.

The 'ends closer to a target' charge rule is now measured against where the model STARTED its charge, not the start of the current hop, so an intermediate sideways step around cover no longer gets wrongly rejected.

Confirming a multi-step charge sends the real multi-point path to the rules engine, so a route you carefully steered around terrain is validated as-drawn (it won't be second-guessed by a straight line cutting back through the terrain you avoided).

releases.summary: Fixed the charge-phase 'Undo Last Model' button doing nothing when clicked. After you dragged a model during a charge, clicking 'Undo Last Model' left the model exactly where you dropped it — the click never reached the button. The charge board handler was swallowing every left-click before the button could react, and only the 'Confirm Charge Moves' button had been excluded from that. 'Undo Last Model' now works: the model snaps back to where it started that charge and re-joins the list of models still to move.
changes: Fixed: the charge-phase 'Undo Last Model' button now responds to a real mouse click. Previously the click was intercepted by the board's model-drag handler and discarded, so nothing happened.
Undoing a model now also resets the 'Used / Left' charge-distance readout, instead of leaving it showing the reverted model's old distance.
Same underlying fix also protects the 'Snap to Contact' button from having a real click swallowed — only 'Confirm Charge Moves' had been guarded before.

releases.summary: Fixed the Consolidate step being blocked by leftover menus. After the last fight of the phase, the 'Fight — Melee Resolution' summary (and, in some flows, an 'Assign Attacks' window) stayed open in the middle of the screen if you hadn't clicked Close on it. When the Consolidate step then began, those windows sat stacked on top of the battlefield, so when you picked a unit to consolidate you couldn't see it to drag it. The game now automatically clears those leftover fight windows the moment the Consolidate step opens, so the unit picker and the bottom-docked Consolidate menu appear over a clear board.
changes: Consolidate step: any leftover 'Fight — Melee Resolution' or 'Assign Attacks' window from the last fight is now dismissed automatically when the Consolidate step begins — you no longer have to hunt for a Close button before you can consolidate.
When you pick a unit to consolidate, the board is now fully visible so you can see and drag that unit's models (the Consolidate menu stays docked at the bottom of the screen as before).
The combat results those windows showed are still available at any time in the right-hand COMBAT LOG panel.

releases.summary: Fixed the confusing Fight phase controls. The right-hand panel used to show a 'MELEE ATTACKS' weapon list with a 'CURRENT TARGETS' box and 'Clear All' / 'Fight!' / 'Auto-Fight' buttons — but that whole set was a dead, leftover way of fighting: selecting a weapon there and clicking an enemy did nothing, and 'Auto-Fight' did nothing either. Meanwhile the real, working Fight flow is the pop-up dialog in the middle of the screen that asks you to pick a fighter and then assign attacks. Having both on screen made it look like the game was broken when you tried the panel. The dead panel controls have been removed, so there is now a single, working way to fight: the pop-up dialogs.
changes: Removed the non-working right-panel 'MELEE ATTACKS' weapon tree, 'CURRENT TARGETS' basket, and the 'Clear All' / 'Fight!' / 'Auto-Fight' buttons from the Fight phase.
Attack allocation is now driven solely by the centre-screen pop-ups (pick a unit to fight, then assign each weapon to a target and confirm) — the flow that already worked.
The right panel still shows the useful read-outs: the fight sequence, the running phase damage tally, the combat log with dice results, fight status, and the Pile In / Consolidate movement buttons.

Under the hood these panel controls never told the game which weapon you'd selected, so the list of valid targets was always empty — which is why clicking an enemy and 'Auto-Fight' both silently did nothing.

releases.summary: Fixed a Fight-phase bug where the game asked YOU to choose targets for the computer's attacks. When the AI's units fought (e.g. you playing Orks vs the AI's Custodes), the 'who do you want to allocate these attacks to?' target-selection dialog popped up for you instead of the AI just picking its own targets and fighting. Cause: the Fight controller only refreshed the 'whose fighter is this?' owner when a human picked a fighter from a dialog, so once one of your units had fought, that owner stayed pointed at you; when the AI's unit fought next, the controller thought the human owned it and showed you the AI's attack-allocation dialog. The controller now updates the owner every time any unit is selected to fight, so the AI silently resolves its own attacks and the dialog only ever appears for your own units.
changes: Fixed: the Fight phase no longer shows you the attack-target dialog for the computer's units. The AI now picks targets and fights with its own units automatically, and you are only asked to allocate attacks for units you control.

Same fix also stops the AI's Pile In and Consolidate prompts from being shown to you — the controller now tracks the active fighter's real owner and hands each dialog to the correct player as control alternates back and forth during the Fight phase.

releases.summary: Added a new pickable army: the Orks 'Battlewagons' list (2000 pts, Rollin' Deff / Speedwaaagh! themed). It shows up in the Player 1/Player 2 army dropdowns like any other list and is ready to deploy and play. The list is a grot-and-gitz horde built around three Battlewagons (each with Targetin' Gizmos) and a trio of Big Meks, backed by Meks, Burna Boyz, Deffkoptas, Gretchin, Lootas, Stormboyz, Tankbustas and Warbikers. New top-down unit art was drawn for every model type that didn't already have a sprite, so the whole army reads at a glance on the board.
changes: New army list 'Battlewagons' (Orks, 2000 pts) selectable in the main-menu army dropdowns: 3 Big Meks, 3 Meks (one with the Dakkamek enhancement), 3 Battlewagons (each with the Targetin' Gizmos upgrade), 2 Burna Boyz, 2 Deffkoptas, 3 Gretchin, 3 Lootas, Stormboyz, 2 Tankbustas (one carrying a Pulsa Rokkit) and 2 Warbikers.

New top-down sprites drawn in the same Vassal-style as the existing art: Big Mek (power klaw + kustom mega-blasta), Battlewagon (deff rolla, turret, wreckin' ball and tracks on a proper rectangular vehicle base), Burna Boyz (flamer gout), Lootas (oversized three-barrel deffgun) and Tankbustas (rokkit launcha with a bomb squig).
Reused the existing Mek, Gretchin, Stormboyz, Warbikers and Deffkoptas sprites for those units in the new list.

releases.summary: Fixed the Charge phase 'Snap to Contact' button appearing to do nothing. The button (along with 'Undo Last Model') only ever appeared when you were actively moving a charging unit, but once you confirmed that charge — or between charges, or while a Command Re-roll prompt was still open — it was left on screen looking clickable even though there was nothing to move. Clicking it in those states silently did nothing. The Confirm / Snap to Contact / Undo Last Model buttons now hide together the moment a charge is finished, so 'Snap to Contact' is only shown when it will actually move models.
changes: Fixed: the 'Snap to Contact' button no longer lingers on screen after a charge is confirmed (and between charges, and while a Command Re-roll dialog is open). Previously it stayed visible and enabled with nothing to move, so clicking it did nothing.

The 'Snap to Contact' and 'Undo Last Model' buttons now show only while you are actively positioning a charging unit, and 'Snap to Contact' is greyed out once every model is already in engagement range (use 'Confirm Charge Moves' at that point).
If 'Snap to Contact' is ever triggered with nothing to place, it now tells you why instead of doing nothing silently.

releases.summary: Fixed a Fight-phase bug that falsely blocked Pile In and Consolidate moves. When you tried to pile in a unit whose models are numbered from 1 (Warbikers, and most multi-model units), the game could wrongly claim a model 'would overlap' with one of its own squadmates — comparing the model against its own old position instead of its neighbours — and refuse the move even though the models were nowhere near each other. It could also skip the last model in the unit entirely when checking for overlaps. Pile In and Consolidate now check each model against the right neighbours, so legitimate moves are accepted and only genuine base overlaps are rejected.
changes: Fixed the phantom 'Model mN would overlap with <unit>/N' error during Pile In and Consolidate. The overlap check was mis-matching model IDs to positions (an off-by-one caused by the models being numbered from 1), so it compared a moving model against its own stale position and rejected valid moves.
The last model in a unit is now included in the overlap check instead of being silently skipped.
The same ID-matching fix was applied to the 'already in base contact' and post-consolidation fight-eligibility checks, which shared the bug.

Note on base-to-base: a model still shows the red 'B2B' lock only when its base is genuinely within the 0.25" contact tolerance of an enemy. Remember a model's BASE (the full oval/circle outline), not the smaller model artwork, is what counts for contact — a bike's long oval base can be touching an enemy even when the motorcycle art looks clear of it.

releases.summary: Fixed 'End Fight Phase' ending the fight for your opponent too. When you clicked End Fight Phase, the game forfeited every remaining combat — including your opponent's units that were still owed a fight — and jumped straight to consolidation. Now, ending the Fight phase only stops YOUR own units from fighting; your opponent still gets to fight all of their remaining eligible units (core rule 12.04: when one player stops selecting, the other fights all their remaining units). The confirmation warning also lists only your own unfought units now, not your opponent's.
changes: Fixed: clicking 'End Fight Phase' no longer forfeits your opponent's fights. Only your own remaining units stop fighting — the opponent's eligible units are handed the Fight step and their selection dialog re-opens so they can fight.

The 'End Fight Phase?' warning now lists only the units YOU would forfeit (your own), instead of also listing the opponent's units as 'won't fight' — which was misleading, since they still get to fight.
Once your opponent has also finished fighting (or has nothing eligible), ending the Fight phase proceeds to the Consolidate step as before.

releases.summary: Fixed the red enemy 'engagement range' circles from the Movement phase lingering on the board after you advanced to the Shooting phase. Those red rings are only there to show where your models can't move during your Movement phase; they now clear away the moment the phase ends, so the Shooting phase board is no longer cluttered by them.
changes: Fixed: the red engagement-range rings drawn around enemy units during the Movement phase (the 'don't move here' circles) no longer stay on the board once you move on to the Shooting phase — they are cleared when the Movement phase ends.
The same cleanup also removes the leftover blue move-range and coherency overlays that could linger past the Movement phase, so nothing movement-only survives into the next phase.

releases.summary: The melee (Fight) resolution window now shows dice as proper d6 face icons everywhere, instead of a mix of plain numbers in the Combat Log and numbers-in-boxes on the Command Re-roll row. The pip faces match the dice used in the Shooting resolution log and the animated dice roller, so dice look consistent across the whole game — gold for 6s, red for 1s, green/red for pass/fail against the target number.
changes: Fight phase Combat Log: hit, wound, save and Feel No Pain rolls now render as inline d6 face icons (rounded square + pips) rather than bracketed number lists like [ 1 5 5 3 6 6 4 4 2 ].
Command Re-roll row: each clickable die now shows its face icon instead of a number in a square; hover it to see the value and re-roll it.
Dice colours follow the same rules as the rest of the game: 6 = gold (critical), 1 = red (fumble), and otherwise green when the roll passes its target and red when it fails.
Command Re-roll results in the log now show the original face → the new face as icons too.

releases.summary: Fixed the confusing 'consolidate failed — skipping movement' messages in the Fight phase. When a unit had no legal Consolidation move — most commonly right after it wiped out the only enemy it was fighting, with no other enemy or objective within 3" — the AI would still try to shuffle it toward the nearest objective (even one 10"+ away), the rules would correctly reject the move, and the log filled with alarming 'consolidate failed' / 'validation failed' lines. The unit still couldn't legally move (that part was correct 10th-edition behaviour: Consolidation must end closer to an enemy within 3" or an objective within 3"), but the game now recognises that up front and the unit simply holds position — no scary 'failed' spam.
changes: Consolidation (12.08): a unit that has no enemy within 3" and no objective within 3" now cleanly holds position instead of logging 'consolidate failed — skipping movement'. This is the expected outcome after you kill the last enemy you were fighting and nothing else is nearby — Consolidation is not a free 3" move, it must head toward an enemy or objective in range.

The AI's consolidation targeting now uses the same 3" range gates as the rules validator (enemy within 3", or objective within 3"), so it no longer attempts moves toward out-of-range objectives that were always going to be rejected.

When it IS your unit's turn to consolidate, the picker still lists it and labels units that can't move with '[No move possible from here]', so you can see at a glance why a unit holds position. If the computer is moving/consolidating your units on its own turn, make sure you're on the latest build — a recent fix (0.42.2) hands your half of every Fight-phase step back to you.

releases.summary: Fixed a bug that broke the Movement phase completely: when you entered Movement, the right-hand unit list was empty and clicking your models did nothing, so you couldn't move anyone. This was caused by a coding error introduced with the 11th-edition coherency update (0.41.1) that stopped the Movement controller from loading. Movement now works again — the unit list populates, and selecting a unit (from the list or by clicking its models on the board) starts its move as before.
changes: Fixed: entering the Movement phase showed an empty right-hand panel and ignored clicks on your models, making it impossible to move units. This affected every game, and was most visible after loading a save.
Root cause was a leftover reference to a variable removed during the 0.41.1 coherency migration, which made the whole Movement controller script fail to load.

releases.summary: Fixed: when the AI charged one of your units, the computer would then pile in (move) your own charged unit for you during the Fight phase, so you never got control of it. Now, on the AI's turn, the AI only ever piles in, fights and consolidates its OWN units — every time it is your half of the Pile In, Fight or Consolidate step, the game waits for you and shows your dialog instead of the AI acting for you.
changes: Fixed: the AI no longer takes your Pile In move for you after charging your unit — your Pile In dialog now appears and waits for you to position your models.
Same fix applies across the whole Fight phase: the AI will not select/attack with your fighters, nor consolidate your units, during your half of a step — it idles until you act.
The AI still plays its own half of each Fight-phase step normally, and the phase hands back and forth correctly between you and the AI (no stalls).

releases.summary: The colored squad-identifying ring on each unit/model token is now drawn BELOW the model art instead of on top of it. Previously the ring painted over the sprite (e.g. a thick green band across the top of the Stompa), which looked wrong; now the sprite renders on top and the ring reads as a band peeking out around the base perimeter.
changes: Unit Color 'Ring Only' mode: the squad color ring is now rendered beneath the token's top-down art/letter, so the model sprite is no longer obscured by the ring. The ring still hugs the base perimeter where the art is mostly transparent, so it stays visible for telling squads apart.

releases.summary: Every secondary-mission unit picker now names duplicate squads clearly AND lets you click the target on the battlefield — extending the Beacon, Marked for Death and Punishment (Condemn) pickers to match Burden of Trust and A Tempting Target. Duplicate squads read like the roster ('Boyz (20 models)' / 'Boyz (10 models)'), and each picker has a 'Pick on board' button.
changes: Beacon: the designate-your-beacon list shows the display name + model count (so two 'Boyz' or two 'Warbosses' are distinguishable), and a 'Pick on board' button lets you click your unit on the battlefield to designate it.
Marked for Death: the enemy-unit picker is disambiguated and both steps (Alpha targets, Gamma target) can be chosen by clicking the enemy units on the board.
Punishment / Condemn: the up-to-3 enemy picker is disambiguated and supports multi-select on the board — click each enemy unit to toggle it, then Done.
Oath of Moment's target list is disambiguated the same way.

The 'Pick on board' flow is now one shared system across all these pickers (and A Tempting Target and Burden of Trust): the dialog hides, a banner shows Cancel (plus Done for multi-select), and clicking empty space or an ineligible target is a no-op — never a dead end.

releases.summary: When your opponent draws 'A Tempting Target', you can now designate the No Man's Land objective by clicking its marker directly on the board — not just by picking a name from the list. The dialog's objective names (e.g. 'Objective NML_1') aren't always obvious, so a new 'Pick on board' button hides the dialog, highlights the eligible objectives and lets you click the one you want on the battlefield.
changes: A Tempting Target: added a 'Pick on board (click an objective marker)' button to the objective-selection dialog. It hides the dialog, highlights the No Man's Land objectives and designates whichever marker you click as the Tempting Target.
The name list still works exactly as before — the board pick is an additional option, and a 'Cancel' banner button returns you to the list.
Clicking empty space or a non-eligible objective while picking on the board shows a hint instead of resolving, so a mis-click can't pick the wrong objective.

releases.summary: The measuring tape has been rebuilt into a reliable click-to-measure tool and no longer fights with the chat panel. Press T (its default key) to arm the tape, click a start point then an end point on the board to drop a measured line, and repeat to chain measurements. Right-click or ESC cancels a half-drawn line, pressing T again puts the tape away, and Y still clears every line. A banner across the top of the screen reminds you of these controls while the tape is armed. Chat now lives on C so it no longer steals the T key.
changes: Measuring tape is now a click-to-measure tool: press T to arm it, click a start point, then click an end point to draw a measured line. It stays armed so you can measure again and again.
Fixed: T used to open the chat panel instead of the tape (both were bound to T). The tape now wins T by default, and chat has moved to the C key.
Fixed: you can now stop and cancel a measurement — right-click or ESC cancels a line you're in the middle of drawing, and pressing T again (or ESC when nothing is in progress) puts the tape away.
Clicking to place the end of a line now works (previously the click did nothing); the finished line and its distance label persist on the board until you clear them.

While the tape is armed, a banner at the top of the screen shows the controls (click start/end, right-click/ESC to cancel, Y to clear, T to exit), and the on-board clicks no longer select or move your units.
Press Y at any time to clear all measurement lines. The measuring-tape and clear-measurements keys remain rebindable in Settings → Controls.

releases.summary: Unit coherency is now enforced by the 11th-edition rule (core rules 03.03) everywhere in the game — deployment, movement, advance, scout, charge and fight. A unit is in coherency only while every model is within 2" of at least one other model AND within 9" of EVERY other model in the unit, so spreading a unit (e.g. Warbikers with an attached Deffkilla Wartrike) too far is now blocked with a message that names the exact 9" rule instead of the old vague 'within 2" of mates'.
changes: Coherency now uses the 11th-edition rule across ALL phases: every model must be within 2" of at least one mate AND within 9" of every other model in the unit. The old 10th-edition 'units of 7+ models need two neighbours each' behaviour has been removed from deployment, movement, advance, scout, charge and fight.

Fight and charge coherency were previously under-enforced (they only checked the 2" rule and ignored the 9" spread) — they now apply the full 11th-edition rule, so a unit can no longer legally string itself out beyond 9" during a pile-in, consolidation or charge.

The deploy error now tells you how many models are out of coherency and which rule is broken — e.g. 'Some models are more than 9" apart — pull the unit closer together' or 'Some models are more than 2" from their nearest mate'.

The on-board deployment coherency circles and the live movement coherency indicator now match the deploy/move gates exactly (all use one shared 11th-edition check), so a model that is touching a neighbour but sits more than 9" from a far-flung mate correctly shows out-of-coherency instead of misleading green.
All coherency checks in the game — every phase, the deploy-action validator, the movement UI and the AI's formation planner — now share one single source of truth, so they can never disagree.

releases.summary: Unit colors now display as a ring by default instead of filling the whole base, and duplicate squads of the same unit are automatically given different colors. The new Settings > Visual 'Unit Color' option defaults to 'Ring Only', which keeps a neutral base and paints your chosen color as a ring inside the base edge so the model art stays clear — switch it to 'Full Base' for the original solid-base look. Deploying a second squad of the same name (e.g. a second Boyz) now auto-picks a different color from the first.
changes: Unit colors now show as a ring around the model by default (neutral base, color band just inside the base perimeter) so the model art stays readable.
New Settings > Visual dropdown 'Unit Color' — 'Ring Only' (default) or 'Full Base' (the original solid colored base).
Right-click > Change Color still sets the color; the setting only changes whether it fills the base or shows as a ring. Applies live to every token, no reload needed, and persists between sessions.
